title: Processes for making azelaic acid and derivatives thereof
abstract: Methods of making azelaic acid, or derivatives thereof, from unsaturated monobasic acids or esters are disclosed herein. In some embodiments, the unsaturated monobasic acids or esters are C10-17 unsaturated monobasic acids or esters.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1 . A method for forming azelaic acid or a derivative thereof, the method comprising: providing a reactant composition comprising a compound of formula (I): reacting the compound of formula (I) with an oxidizing agent to form a product position comprising a compound of formula (II): wherein: R 1 is a hydrogen atom, C1-12 alkyl, C1-12 heteroalkyl, or a cationic counterion; R 2 is a hydrogen atom or C1-8 alkyl; and R 3 is a hydrogen atom, C1-12 alkyl, C1-12 heteroalkyl, or a cationic counterion. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein R 1 is a hydrogen atom. 3. The method of claim 1 , wherein R 1 is C1-12 alkyl. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein R 1 is methyl, ethyl, or isopropyl. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein R 1 is methyl. 6. The method of claim 1 , wherein R 1 is C1-12 heteroalkyl, such as C1-12 oxyalkyl. 7. The method of claim 1 , wherein R 1 is a cationic counterion, such as a sodium cation, a potassium cation, or an ammonium cation. 8. The method of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein R 2 is a hydrogen atom. 9. The method of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein R 2 is C1-8 alkyl. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein R 2 is methyl, ethyl, n-propyl, n-butyl, n-pentyl, n-hexyl, n-heptyl, or n-octyl. 1 1 . The method of claim 10, wherein R 2 is methyl or ethyl. 12. The method of claim 1 1 , wherein R 2 is ethyl. 13. The method of any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein R 3 is a hydrogen atom. 14. The method of any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein R 3 is Ci-12 alkyl. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein R 3 is methyl, ethyl, or isopropyl. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein R 3 is methyl. 17. The method of any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein R 3 is C1-12 heteroalkyl, such as Ci-i2 0xyalkyl. 18. The method of any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein R 3 is a cationic counterion, such as a sodium cation, a potassium cation, or an ammonium cation. 19. The method of any one of claims 1 to 18, wherein the oxidizing agent comprises hydrogen peroxide. 20. The method of any one of claims 1 to 18, wherein the oxidizing agent comprises ozone. 21 . The method of any one of claims 1 to 20, further comprising converting the compound of formula (I I) to azelaic acid. 22. The method of claim 21 , wherein the converting comprises hydrolyzing the compound of formula (I I). 23. The method of claim 21 , wherein the converting comprises saponifying the compound of formula (II), and, in some embodiments, followed by acidification of the product of the saponification. 24. A method for forming azelaic acid or a derivative thereof, the method comprising: providing a reactant composition comprising methyl 9-dodecenoate; and reacting the methyl 9-dodecenoate with an oxidizing agent to form 1 -methyl azelate. 25. The method of claim 24, comprising converting the 1 -methyl azelate to azelaic acid. 26. The method of claim 25, wherein the converting comprises hydrolyzing 1 -methyl azelate. 27. The method of claim 25, wherein the converting comprises saponifying 1 -methyl azelate, and, in some embodiments, followed by acidification of the product of the saponification.
